entities.name: vāc uttama
entities.cite: Verse 18 names the goal: the uttama rupa of vac, speech's highest form

entities.name: bheda
entities.cite: bheda has 'set' (astamita, the sun-verb: differentiation gone down like daylight)

entities.name: jyotiḥ
entities.cite: a suddha jyotis that vivartate — the kanda's master-verb again

entities.name: tamasī
entities.cite: within this very darkness of ordinary divided experience

entities.name: aloka
entities.cite: who have stepped beyond the pair aloka-tamasi, light *and* darkness

entities.name: mūrti
entities.cite: those past murti-vyapara-darsana, the seeing of shape and function

entities.name: vyāpāra
entities.cite: the whole apparatus of ordinary cognition

entities.name: darśana
entities.cite: the seeing of shape and function (the whole apparatus of ordinary cognition)

entities.name: nimitta
entities.cite: nimittas of speech (its occasioning apparatus: the phonemes, the aksara-smrti's signs)

entities.name: yoga
entities.cite: by the yoga preceded by the word — union pursued through language itself

entities.name: śabda
entities.cite: sabda-purvena yogena, by the yoga preceded by the word

entities.name: varṇas
entities.cite: the high and low sounds of all four Vedas, Atharvan and Angiras hymns, Saman, Rk, Yajus

entities.name: vedas
entities.cite: the entire audible revelation housed, differentiated, within the undifferentiated

entities.name: prakriyā
entities.cite: divided bahudha by prakriya-bhedas — differences of derivational procedure, the grammarian's own term

entities.name: vyākaraṇa
entities.cite: is *arrived at through grammar*: tad vyakaranam agamya, param brahma adhigamyate

axioms.cite: is *arrived at through grammar*: tad vyakaranam agamya, param brahma adhigamyate

axioms.name: vivartate
axioms.cite: a suddha jyotis that vivartate — the kanda's master-verb again — even asminn eva tamasi, within this very darkness of ordinary divided experience

axioms.name: sabda_purva
axioms.cite: sabda-purvena yogena, by the yoga preceded by the word — union pursued through language itself

axioms.name: asraya
axioms.cite: in that highest speech the uccavaca varnas — the high and low sounds of all four Vedas, Atharvan and Angiras hymns, Saman, Rk, Yajus — hold prthak-sthiti, distinct stations

axioms.name: agama
axioms.cite: is *arrived at through grammar*: tad vyakaranam agamya, param brahma adhigamyate

axioms.name: eka
axioms.cite: that One, divided bahudha by prakriya-bhedas — differences of derivational procedure

denials.name: aloka_matra
denials.cite: the highest form is not the bright member of an opposition but what precedes opposition

denials.name: tamasi_matra
denials.cite: the highest form is not the bright member of an opposition but what precedes opposition

claims.cite: arriving at that through grammar, the supreme Brahman is attained

claims.name: sabda_purva
claims.cite: the yoga that is preceded by the word

claims.name: asraya
claims.cite: in which the high and low sounds of the Atharvans and Angirases, of the Saman, the Rk and the Yajus, hold their separate stations

claims.name: eka
claims.cite: that which, though one, is divided manifold by the differences of derivational procedure

rejected_readings.label: linguistic_structure_reductionism
rejected_readings.rendering: The highest form of speech is merely a linguistic structure that underlies all the world's words.
rejected_readings.why: Reduces vac_uttama from absolute reality to a mere linguistic property. The commentary insists that the ultimate IS language (word-essence), not a linguistic quality of something else. The sort error (property vs absolute) makes the identity claim categorically false.

rejected_readings.label: parinama_transformation
rejected_readings.rendering: Brahman directly transforms itself into the differentiated world through the operation of grammatical derivational procedures.
rejected_readings.why: Asserts parinama (real transformation) instead of vivarta (appearance without loss of unity). The verse emphasizes that param_brahman remains one despite being divided by prakriya-bhedas; it does not undergo real transformation into them.

rejected_readings.label: language_abandonment_path
rejected_readings.rendering: True knowledge of Brahman requires abandoning all language and discursive thought, transcending the word-based approach.
rejected_readings.why: Contradicts the central methodological claim (axiom 3) that yoga is sabda-purva, grounded in and preceded by the word. The path is not away from language but through language itself. The verse's entire architecture emphasizes that the word-led yoga is the means.

rejected_readings.label: vedas_separate_realism
rejected_readings.rendering: The Vedic sounds and derivational procedures are distinct realities that merely reflect upon or approximate the highest speech, but are not fundamentally identical with it.
rejected_readings.why: Contradicts axiom 4 (varnas asrita vac_uttama). The verse is explicit that the sounds of all four Vedas hold their stations IN that highest speech, not outside it. They are not separate reflections but housed within the undifferentiated One.

notes: The five-verse passage (1.18-22) is a single sentence building systematically: verse 18 names the goal (uttama rupa of vac), 19 the worshippers (those transcending murti-vyapara-darsana and aloka-tamasi opposition), 20 the method (sabda-purvena yoga), 21 the cosmic ground (Vedic varnas housed in highest speech), and 22 the discipline (vyakarana as the path to param brahman). The central ontological claim is that vac_uttama = param_brahman (absolute, not property). The relation vivartate encodes manifestation without transformation. The key innovation is making yoga and vyakarana explicit powers that operate through language toward the absolute. The verse resolves the tension between One and Many through vivarta: the one param_brahman appears as divided by prakriya-bhedas yet remains undivided.
